summary: Significant gaps: §13 is a thin "fresh local install" sketch that leaves the production deploy spine under-built — no signing/notarization resolution (OQ-001), no GBrain version-pin upgrade gate, no migration rollback/recovery, no doctor/repair command, no process supervision/restart policy, no secrets bootstrap, and no app upgrade path.

findings.finding: GBrain version-pin and its UPGRADE gate are entirely absent from the architecture. The PRD makes pinning a Phase-0 gate and the compat contract version-bound, so bumping the pin is a real deploy operation (re-run compat contract + re-index from Markdown) — yet the draft never names where the SHA lives or how a pin upgrade is gated.
findings.evidence: PRD §17 Phase 0 'GBrain known-good version selected and pinned (SHA recorded in repo config)' and Phase 2 'GBrain compatibility contract test green against the pinned version' (system_of_work_assistant_prd_v0_3.md:1020,1025); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §13 (lines 335-350) and §17 (392-422) mention neither GBrain pinning nor an upgrade gate.
findings.proposed_fix: Add to §13: record the pinned GBrain SHA in repo config; the GBrain process must verify the running version matches the pin at startup (mismatch ⇒ block/degrade + System Health). Define the pin-upgrade gate: bump SHA only after the §12 GBrain compatibility/parity contract suite is green and a full re-index-from-Markdown succeeds.

findings.finding: Migration apply/ROLLBACK and partial-migration recovery are undefined. §4 only says a migration mismatch 'blocks worker and reports repair instructions' — there is no down-migration/rollback, no backup-before-migrate, no behavior when a migration fails mid-apply (partial schema), and no app-version<->schema-version compatibility check on upgrade. Drizzle is forward-only by default; the operational store holds load-bearing audit/approvals/outbox state, so a failed migration with no recovery is an under-built production concern.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §4 'Migration mismatch: startup blocks worker and reports repair instructions' (line 158); §12 lists only 'Drizzle migration/repository contract tests' (line 321) — no rollback/recovery test. OQ focus explicitly names 'migration apply/rollback'.
findings.proposed_fix: Specify in §13: backup operational DB before applying migrations; migrations run transactionally where the engine allows; on failed/partial apply, restore from the pre-migration backup and refuse to start with a typed repair message; record app-version<->schema-version compatibility and refuse to run a newer schema against older app code.

findings.finding: No doctor/repair command is specified anywhere in the architecture, despite RISKS naming a 'doctor command' as a primary mitigation and §4 referencing 'repair instructions'. The architecture defines neither the command, its health checks, nor what it can repair (DB, Temporal, GBrain pin, secrets, loopback ports).
findings.evidence: RISKS.md RISK-013 mitigation 'Clean install test from Phase 1, doctor command, presets, docs' (line 19); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §4 'reports repair instructions' (158) and §13 (335-350) define no such command.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a §13 'doctor/repair' subsection: a single command that checks worker/Temporal/GBrain liveness, schema/migration state, GBrain pin match, Keychain secret presence, loopback port availability, and Markdown<->GBrain parity; outputs typed findings and offers bounded auto-repairs (re-index GBrain from Markdown, re-bind ports, re-run pending migration after backup).

findings.finding: Process supervision/restart policy for the three long-lived local services (control-plane worker, local Temporal dev server, GBrain process/sidecar) is unstated. §13 says main 'supervises' the worker and GBrain runs as a 'process/sidecar', but there is no restart policy, crash backoff, crash-loop handling, or liveness/health probe — and no defined behavior when the Temporal server or GBrain process dies (degrade? restart? surface System Health?). LIFE-1 lease + Temporal resume cover workflow durability, not OS-process supervision.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §13 lines 339-342 ('supervises control-plane worker', 'GBrain runs as single-owner local process/sidecar per workspace brain'); §4 only covers 'DB unavailable' degraded mode (157). No restart/backoff/liveness spec.
findings.proposed_fix: Define a supervision tree in §13: main owns worker; worker owns/monitors Temporal dev server and GBrain processes; specify restart-with-exponential-backoff, a crash-loop ceiling that flips the app to degraded mode + System Health item, and liveness probes for each service.

findings.finding: Secrets bootstrap on a fresh install is unstated. §13 says connectors use Keychain-stored credentials, but there is no first-run bootstrap: how the Keychain service/namespace is created, what happens when required secrets are absent (provider/connector disabled and surfaced vs hard fail), and how an unsigned build-from-source obtains Keychain access. This is on the clean-install acceptance path.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §13 'External connectors use user-configured credentials stored in Keychain' (line 343); §5 KeychainSecretsAdapter referenced but no bootstrap flow; PRD §20.1 'Open-source install' / REQ-NF-005 require fresh-install success (REQUIREMENTS.md:33).
findings.proposed_fix: Add a §13 first-run secrets bootstrap: create the Keychain service namespace, run with zero secrets by default (each missing-secret provider/connector starts disabled and is surfaced in System Health rather than crashing), and document how Keychain access is granted for an unsigned from-source build.

findings.finding: §13 omits the signing/notarization decision entirely; it lives only as an unresolved open question. Production-grade install must record the V1 packaging target and Gatekeeper handling. OQ-001's documented default is 'unsigned local build, document the unsigned path'.
findings.evidence: OPEN_QUESTIONS.md OQ-001 'What exact Electron packaging/signing/notarization target is required for V1? ... Document unsigned local build path' (line 9); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §13 (335-350) says nothing about signing, notarization, or Gatekeeper quarantine.
findings.proposed_fix: In §13 state the V1 default: ships as an unsigned build-from-source (no Developer ID signing / notarization); document the Gatekeeper quarantine implication (xattr quarantine removal / right-click-open) for users; signed+notarized DMG distribution is explicitly deferred to V1.1.

findings.finding: Packaging target for V1 is owner-load-bearing and unresolved (OQ-001): is an unsigned build-from-source acceptable for ALL V1 users, or is a signed + notarized distributable required for any of them? This decides whether an Apple Developer ID + notarization pipeline is in V1 scope.
findings.evidence: OPEN_QUESTIONS.md OQ-001 (line 9) marks this open with resolution deferred to '/arch-finalize or install spike'; no DECISIONS.md entry locks a packaging/signing target.
findings.proposed_fix: Owner confirms: unsigned build-from-source only for V1 (notarization deferred) vs a signed/notarized DMG in V1 scope. Do not invent — record the answer in DECISIONS.md and §13.

findings.finding: There is no app UPGRADE path — §13 covers fresh install only. A production OSS app needs an upgrade runbook and a statement of what state survives an upgrade.
findings.evidence: ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §13 'V1 local' bullets (337-344) and §15 deferred list (361-373) describe install but no upgrade; PRD §17 Phase 10 is 'Install hardening' (line 1033), still install-only.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a §13 upgrade runbook: git pull -> pnpm install -> backup + drizzle migrate -> GBrain pin check/re-index if SHA changed -> restart supervised services; enumerate surviving state (Markdown via git, operational DB migrated, Temporal history, GBrain index rebuildable from Markdown).

findings.finding: The packaging toolchain is unspecified and the PRD phase plan still names the obsolete 'Tauri' bundler. With Electron locked, §13/§17 should name the Electron packaging toolchain that produces the distributable.
findings.evidence: PRD §17 Phase 1 still lists 'Tauri' among components kept green (system_of_work_assistant_prd_v0_3.md:1024) and §11 'built with Tauri' (line 410); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §17 tooling list (415-422) names pnpm/Turbo/Drizzle but no Electron packager.
findings.proposed_fix: Reconcile the stale Tauri wording and specify the Electron packaging toolchain (e.g. electron-builder or electron-forge) producing the local DMG/app bundle; note it replaces Tauri bundling throughout.

findings.finding: Backup/restore (disaster-recovery posture) for the non-Markdown stores is unstated. Markdown is git-backed and GBrain is rebuildable from Markdown, but the operational SQLite/Postgres store holds load-bearing audit/approvals/outbox/cursor state whose loss is not recoverable from Markdown, and Temporal persistent state is not addressed.
findings.evidence: DECISIONS.md notes 'Git backup' only for workspace Markdown (line 18); ARCHITECTURE_DRAFT.md §4 lists operational store contents 'events, audit, approvals, outboxes, connector cursors...' (144) but §13 gives no backup/restore for it or Temporal state.
findings.proposed_fix: State a backup/restore posture in §13: which stores are reconstructible (GBrain from Markdown), which need explicit backup (operational DB audit/approvals/outbox), and the restore steps after disk loss or corruption.
